identity: NOVA Strategy Core — AI-native execution intelligence layer

personal_trading_window.note: Pedro's active trading window — no new entries after 11:00 ET by default

london_close_window.note: Outside personal window — flag but do not initiate

pros_rules.description: PROS = directional continuation execution. IB defines the session draw → manipulation leg establishes bias → OTE pullback → rejection confirmation → continuation to IB target.
ib_pre_check: Lock IB high, low, and midpoint once the 9:30–10:30 ET window closes (or use developing range before close)
Determine directional draw: is price targeting IB high (bullish) or IB low (bearish)?
Only evaluate PROS setups that align with the IB draw — countertrend setups against the IB draw are downgraded or avoided

core_concepts.expansion: Impulsive directional move establishing intent and range
core_concepts.displacement: Aggressive break through a level, leaving order blocks / FVG behind. NOT the retracement destination.
core_concepts.fib_retracement: Price pulls back INTO the fib zone (0.382–0.618 of expansion). This is the entry area.
core_concepts.rejection_confirmation: Price taps fib zone, shows rejection signal (pin bar, engulf, momentum shift). Not just touch.
core_concepts.order_blocks: Form of displacement evidence — area of significant supply/demand left by displacement candle. Acts as confluence, not entry trigger.
core_concepts.defended_pullback: Key PROS confirmation: pullback into fib is absorbed cleanly, price defends level and resumes direction.

long_setup.entry_signal: rejection candle or momentum reversal at fib
long_setup.target: expansion high or next liquidity above
long_setup.stop: below fib zone / below displacement base

short_setup.entry_signal: rejection candle or momentum reversal at fib
short_setup.target: expansion low or next liquidity below
short_setup.stop: above fib zone / above displacement high

invalidation: Price closes beyond displacement base (expansion origin violated)
Retracement exceeds 0.786 fib without clear structural reason
Multiple failed rejections at fib zone
Setup forms inside dead zone (12:00–14:00 ET)

quality_grade.A: IB draw confirmed + manipulation leg clear + OTE tap at 0.618–0.705 + strong rejection + session timing inside primary window
quality_grade.B: IB draw confirmed + most criteria met but one element unclear, weak, or slightly outside OTE zone
quality_grade.C: IB draw ambiguous OR two or more execution criteria weak — reduced confidence
quality_grade.D: No IB draw established, setup opposes IB direction, or two+ hard criteria missing — do not trade

orb_rules.description: ORB = auction/rejection execution. Opening range defines the balance zone. Entries based on ownership shifts and liquidity sweeps.

range_construction.description: ORB high, low, and midpoint are locked from the first 15-minute candle of pre-market. No execution during this window — range construction only.

execution_phase.description: NY open begins. Auction interpretation starts. Watch for breakout direction and retest of ORB midpoint.

cutoff.description: No new ORB entries after 11:00 ET. ORB edge is considered degraded. Opening auction logic is only valid during the active NY morning session.

orb_definition.orb_mid: Midpoint of opening range — (high + low) / 2 — critical execution level

entry_1_midpoint_rejection.name: Midpoint Rejection
sequence: Price breaks out of ORB (above high or below low)
Entry: continuation of original breakout direction
entry_1_midpoint_rejection.target: Opposite side of ORB box, then extension

entry_2_liquidity_rejection.name: Untapped Liquidity Rejection
sequence: Price taps external liquidity beyond ORB (stops above prior high or below prior low)
Entry: back toward ORB midpoint as first target
entry_2_liquidity_rejection.target: ORB midpoint, then opposite ORB extreme

invalidation: Entry attempted before 09:30 ET (execution phase not open)
Entry attempted after 11:00 ET (ORB cutoff — edge degraded)
Price reclaims ORB from wrong side with conviction (full candle body close beyond mid)
Multiple ORB violations in both directions (balance breakdown)
News event within 30min of setup trigger
ORB range excessively wide (>15pts MES) — reduced reliability

quality_grade.A: Clean ORB definition + clear breakout at/after 09:30 + clean retest + sharp midpoint rejection + before 10:30 ET
quality_grade.B: Criteria met but one element late, slow, or slightly sloppy
quality_grade.C: Marginal — two or more elements weak

no_trade_conditions: Major macro event within 30 minutes (FOMC, CPI, NFP, GDP)
Session outside personal trading window (post-11:00 ET)
Daily loss at or beyond 2% of account — session over
Considering Trade 2 after a loss or break-even on Trade 1 — not permitted
Prior stop-out on same setup in same session — mandatory pause
Setup quality grade C or below
Conflicting signals: PROS setup in ORB context without resolution of ORB
Choppy/overlapping price action — no clear expansion present
Already in a position (single-position architecture — no stacking)
No clean setup present — do nothing. We don't trade because we have to, we trade because we can.

risk_management.second_trade_risk: 0.5% of account — Trade 2 only, and only after Trade 1 was a win
risk_management.second_trade_condition: Trade 2 is only permitted after a winning Trade 1. A loss or break-even on Trade 1 ends the session.
risk_management.max_daily_loss: 2% of account — session is over, no further entries
risk_management.position_sizing: Size is calculated from the 1% risk rule and the defined stop distance — not from a fixed contract count
risk_management.trade_limit: Maximum 2 trades per session — Trade 2 requires a prior win and uses half the normal risk

tp_management.tp1: IB high or low — first logical checkpoint, not a mandatory exit
tp_management.tp1_hold_criteria: If volume is still strong and momentum intact at TP1, hold the trade — do not cut it short
tp_management.tp1_exit_criteria: Volume weakening or rejection forming at TP1 — take the win
tp_management.extended_target: 3:1 RR minimum when holding past TP1, ideally 4:1 RR
tp_management.trail_trigger: When price breaks a structural level in the trade direction, trail stop to that level (near-zero risk)
tp_management.philosophy: IB high/low is the first target, not the ceiling. Same principle as ORB — TP1 is a checkpoint, not a forced exit.

grading_criteria: Entry timing relative to rejection candle (immediate vs. chased)
Stop placement discipline (rule-based vs. discretionary widening)
TP management discipline (held at TP1 when momentum warranted vs. exited prematurely)
Session timing score (inside 09:30–11:00 ET personal window = full credit)
No-trade condition check (all clear = full credit)

alert_timing.pre_session_check: 09:15 ET — macro scan, bias check, watchlist prep
alert_timing.orb_construction_window: 08:00–08:15 ET — ORB range locked (high, low, midpoint defined — NO execution)
alert_timing.execution_phase_open: 09:30 ET — NY open, auction interpretation begins, ORB entries now valid
alert_timing.orb_cutoff: 11:00 ET — no new ORB entries after this time, edge considered degraded
alert_timing.cutoff: 11:00 ET — no new entries after unless explicit condition met
alert_timing.alert_expiry: Alerts expire after 15 minutes if not triggered

pine_role.description: Pine Script is the visual helper and chart annotation layer. It is NOT the decision brain.
pine_responsibilities: Chart annotation: expansion labels, entry zone highlights

on_connect: Call chart_get_state to read current symbol, timeframe, indicator list
Scan for active PROS or ORB setup conditions
chart_analysis_sequence: 1. Get chart state (symbol, TF, current time)
3. Identify current session and whether IB window has closed
4. Lock IB high, low, midpoint — determine directional draw (IB high or IB low)
5. Check ORB range if within NY open window (09:30–11:00 ET)
6. Identify manipulation leg if present — does it align with IB draw?
7. Apply PROS rules: check OTE zone, rejection confirmation, IB alignment
8. Apply ORB rules if applicable (E1 or E2)
9. Grade setup using IB-anchored quality criteria
10. Report: setup type, grade, IB draw direction, entry zone, target, stop, session status, no-trade flags
